perturbation.original_text: Furthermore, the Receiving Party hereby undertakes: (a) not to use the AMDOCS Confidential Information for any purposes other than performance of the Services; (b) not to sell, grant, make available to, or otherwise allow the use of the AMDOCS Confidential Information by any third party, directly or indirectly; and (c) not to use, directly or indirectly, the AMDOCS Confidential Information in the development and/or sale of software systems, for itself or for a third party, and/or in the provision of any services to a third party, except for the Services to be provided by the Receiving Party to SBC.
perturbation.changed_text: Furthermore, the Receiving Party hereby undertakes: (a) to use the AMDOCS Confidential Information for any purposes other than performance of the Services; (b) to sell, grant, make available to, or otherwise allow the use of the AMDOCS Confidential Information by any third party, directly or indirectly; and (c) to use, directly or indirectly, the AMDOCS Confidential Information in the development and/or sale of software systems, for itself or for a third party, and/or in the provision of any services to a third party, except for the Services to be provided by the Receiving Party to SBC.
perturbation.explanation: The original section explicitly prohibits the use, sale, or any form of allowance of usage of AMDOCS Confidential Information by any third party or any other purpose apart from performance of services. However, by deleting the word 'not', the modified text directly contradicts the original clauses (a), (b), and (c). This directly permits the Receiving Party to use AMDOCS confidential information for any purpose and to sell/grant it to third parties, which clashes with the NDA's fundamental purpose. Therefore, the added deletion creates an in-text contradiction within the section, and with the overall aim of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Furthermore, the Receiving Party hereby undertakes: (a) to use the AMDOCS Confidential Information for any purposes other than performance of the Services; (b) to sell, grant, make available to, or otherwise allow the use of the AMDOCS Confidential Information by any third party, directly or indirectly; and (c) to use, directly or indirectly, the AMDOCS Confidential Information in the development and/or sale of software systems, for itself or for a third party, and/or in the provision of any services to a third party, except for the Services to be provided by the Receiving Party to SBC.
perturbation.justification: The original text prevents the receiving party from using or sharing AMDOCS confidential information for any purpose other than performing services. The changed text, by removing 'not,' explicitly allows the receiving party to use and share the information for any purpose, creating a direct and irreconcilable conflict within the document. The Contradicted Text is identical to the Changed Text, which proves there is an In-Text contradiction. This contradiction undermines the core confidentiality obligations and renders the NDA ineffective.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the termination or expiration of this Agreement for any reason or upon the conclusion of the Services and/or at the request of AMDOCS, the Receiving Party shall: (a) return to AMDOCS any document or other material in tangible form in its possession being part of the AMDOCS Confidential Information; and (b) destroy any document or other material in tangible form that contains the AMDOCS Confidential Information together with confidential and/or proprietary information of a third party, and confirm such destruction in writing to AMDOCS.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the termination or expiration of this Agreement for any reason or upon the conclusion of the Services and/or at the request of AMDOCS, the Receiving Party shall: (a) retain any document or other material in tangible form in its possession being part of the AMDOCS Confidential Information; and (b) preserve any document or other material in tangible form that contains the AMDOCS Confidential Information together with confidential and/or proprietary information of a third party, without confirming such preservation in writing to AMDOCS.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'return to AMDOCS' to 'retain' and 'destroy' to 'preserve', and removing the requirement for written confirmation, the modified text creates a direct contradiction. The original clause mandates the return or destruction of confidential information upon termination, ensuring AMDOCS's proprietary rights are protected. The new clause mandates the opposite – retention and preservation – completely undermining the purpose of the NDA. This contradiction is central to the purpose of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon the termination or expiration of this Agreement for any reason or upon the conclusion of the Services and/or at the request of AMDOCS, the Receiving Party shall: (a) retain any document or other material in tangible form in its possession being part of the AMDOCS Confidential Information; and (b) preserve any document or other material in tangible form that contains the AMDOCS Confidential Information together with confidential and/or proprietary information of a third party, without confirming such preservation in writing to AMDOCS.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates the return or destruction of confidential information, but the changed text requires the retention and preservation of the same information. This is a direct and irreconcilable conflict. The contradiction text provided is the same as the Changed Text, highlighting that the contradiction arises from the modification itself.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party acknowledges that a breach of this Agreement may cause AMDOCS extensive and irreparable harm and damage, and agrees that AMDOCS shall be entitled to injunctive relief to prevent use or disclosure of its Proprietary Information not authorized by this Agreement, in addition to any other remedy available to AMDOCS under applicable law.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party acknowledges that a breach of this Agreement will not cause AMDOCS any harm or damage, and agrees that AMDOCS shall not be entitled to injunctive relief to prevent use or disclosure of its Proprietary Information not authorized by this Agreement, nor any other remedy available to AMDOCS under applicable law.
perturbation.explanation: The original text asserts that a breach of the NDA can cause irreparable harm to AMDOCS, entitling them to injunctive relief and other legal remedies. In contrast, the modified text denies the possibility of harm from a breach and removes AMDOCS's right to injunctive relief or any other remedy. This creates a direct contradiction. The text is in contradiction with the overall spirit of the document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party acknowledges that a breach of this Agreement will not cause AMDOCS any harm or damage, and agrees that AMDOCS shall not be entitled to injunctive relief to prevent use or disclosure of its Proprietary Information not authorized by this Agreement, nor any other remedy available to AMDOCS under applicable law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that a breach of the agreement will not cause AMDOCS any harm or damage and that AMDOCS is not entitled to injunctive relief or any other remedy. The contradicted text mirrors the changed text, which, according to the perturbation explanation, contradicts the overall spirit of the document that a breach could cause irreparable harm and entitle AMDOCS to remedies. The modification creates a direct in-text contradiction by negating AMDOCS's rights and remedies in case of a breach, where previously they were entitled to injunctive relief and other legal remedies.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes any prior or contemporaneous oral or written representation with regard to the subject matter hereof. This Agreement may not be modified except by a written instrument signed by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement does not constitute the entire agreement between the parties and does not supersede any prior or contemporaneous oral or written representation with regard to the subject matter hereof. This Agreement may be modified without a written instrument signed by both parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause establishes that the written agreement is the complete and final agreement, superseding any previous discussions and that any modifications must be in writing and signed by both parties. The modified version explicitly states that this agreement is not the entire agreement, does not supersede prior agreements, and can be modified informally. This creates a direct in-text contradiction. The change directly contradicts the principle of contract law of 'entire agreement', this directly impacts the legal certainty, and it creates a very high impact structural flaw.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement does not constitute the entire agreement between the parties and does not supersede any prior or contemporaneous oral or written representation with regard to the subject matter hereof. This Agreement may be modified without a written instrument signed by both parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text, because it states that the agreement 'does not constitute the entire agreement' and 'can be modified without a written instrument,' while the original agreement, by stating the opposite, created a legally binding document that can't be altered without a written instrument. The contradictory text is identical to the changed text, so the impact is doubled.
